req.title: Diagnostic report (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a DiagnosticReport must reference at least one Observation (positive path), must be rejected with 422 when the reference list is empty (negative path), and boundary behaviour must be verified at exactly one Observation reference (minimum valid count). Additional negative paths cover permission denial (non-clinician role) and missing mandatory element (status field). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up state, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations
preconditions: At least one Observation resource exists in the system
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a clinician and obtain a bearer token
Create an Observation resource to obtain a valid Observation ID
POST /DiagnosticReport with status='final', subject reference, and result array containing the Observation reference
expected: Response body id field is non-empty
result array in response matches submitted references
trace: R-HLT-062: clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations(
    clinician_auth_headers, create_observation, fhir_base_url, cleanup_resource
):
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_auth_headers)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "General Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
    assert body.get("id")
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) == 1
    cleanup_resource("DiagnosticReport", body["id"], clinician_auth_headers)

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_array_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a clinician and obtain a bearer token
POST /DiagnosticReport with status='final' but result array set to empty list
expected: Response body contains an error indicating missing Observation references
trace: R-HLT-062: missing Observation links shall be rejected
R-HLT-062: error pathway - 422 when references empty
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_array_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_headers, fhir_base_url
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "General Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "result" in str(body).lower() or "observation" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation_boundary
preconditions: Exactly one Observation resource will be created for the test
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a clinician and obtain a bearer token
POST /DiagnosticReport with result array containing exactly that one Observation reference
expected: Response result array contains exactly one entry
DiagnosticReport id is present in response
trace: R-HLT-062: missing Observation links shall be rejected (lower boundary: 0 fails, 1 succeeds)
R-HLT-062: BVA on minimum valid cardinality of result array
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation_boundary(
    clinician_auth_headers, create_observation, fhir_base_url, cleanup_resource
):
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_auth_headers)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Boundary Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) == 1
    cleanup_resource("DiagnosticReport", body["id"], clinician_auth_headers)

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_denied_for_non_clinician_role
preconditions: A user exists with role: patient (non-clinician)
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a patient (non-clinician) and obtain a bearer token
Create an Observation ID via a privileged setup fixture
POST /DiagnosticReport with valid payload using the patient token
trace: R-HLT-062: precondition role:clinician; non-clinician must be denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_denied_for_non_clinician_role(
    patient_auth_headers, clinician_auth_headers,
    create_observation, fhir_base_url
):
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_auth_headers)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Unauthorized Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=patient_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_create_diagnostic_report_missing_status_returns_error
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a clinician and obtain a bearer token
POST /DiagnosticReport omitting the mandatory 'status' field entirely
expected: Response body references the missing 'status' field
trace: R-HLT-062: missing mandatory elements must be tested (negative path)
R-HLT-062: DT row - valid references but missing required status field
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_diagnostic_report_missing_status_returns_error(
    clinician_auth_headers, create_observation, fhir_base_url
):
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_auth_headers)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "code": {"text": "No Status Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "status" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_finalize_report_from_preliminary_state_transition
preconditions: A DiagnosticReport in status='preliminary' already exists
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: Authenticate as a clinician and obtain a bearer token
POST /DiagnosticReport with status='preliminary' and the Observation reference
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status='preliminary'
trace: R-HLT-062: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-062: STC preliminary -> final valid transition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_finalize_report_from_preliminary_state_transition(
    clinician_auth_headers, create_observation, fhir_base_url, cleanup_resource
):
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_auth_headers)
    create_payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "code": {"text": "Preliminary Report"},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=create_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    report_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    update_payload = {**create_payload, "id": report_id, "status": "final"}
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DiagnosticReport/{report_id}",
        json=update_payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    assert put_resp.json().get("status") == "final"
    cleanup_resource("DiagnosticReport", report_id, clinician_auth_headers)
